interpretation:
WARNING: Platt slope a=-1.08 is negative -- slope estimate is unreliable. This indicates the win_prob values are too narrowly clustered for Platt to fit a meaningful slope (selection filter truncates the distribution). Do NOT apply the Platt slope correction at this sample size. Use the bias correction only (see below).
  Systematic bias: model over-predicts by 11.7%. Actionable: shift all win_prob values by -0.1168 as a flat correction until slope becomes estimable. Partial explanation: selection filter (only posting picks above a win_prob threshold) is expected to create upward truncation bias.
  Brier score 0.2593 >= 0.23 target. With n=76 and a narrow win_prob range, this may reflect small-sample variance. Re-evaluate at n=300 with a wider win_prob spread.
  n=76: Platt scaling is appropriate at this sample size but slope is unreliable with a truncated distribution. Upgrade to isotonic regression at n>=300. Track Brier score monthly; flag if it drifts >10pct month-over-month.
